title: EPIC Launches Brandt Research Fellowship and 2026–27 Pre-Doctoral Research Professional Roles in Climate & Energy Economics
summary: The Energy Policy Institute at the University of Chicago (EPIC) has announced a major expansion of its early-career research opportunities with the launch of the Brandt Research Fellowship , alongside multiple full-time pre-doctoral Research Professional positions for the 2026 27 academic cycle. These opportunities are designed to strengthen global research and policy impact in climate change, energy markets, and environmental economics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The Brandt Research Fellowship is supported by a generous gift from Ted Brandt, MBA 87, founder and CEO of Marathon Capital, a clean-economy-focused advisory firm. The fellowship operates within EPIC s highly regarded Pre-Doctoral Fellows Program, which serves as a bridge between undergraduate or master s education and doctoral study. Brandt emphasized the importance of rigorous energy research in today s global economy, noting that energy economics and geopolitics influence nearly every sector. Through this fellowship, EPIC continues to ask critical policy questions and deliver evidence-based solutions that shape decision-making worldwide. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About the EPIC Pre-Doctoral Fellows Program Brandt Research Fellows join EPIC for one or two years, working closely with EPIC-affiliated faculty and researchers. Fellows gain hands-on experience across all stages of the research process, including project management, data collection and analysis, manuscript preparation, and policy engagement. Research spans energy markets, climate impacts, environmental externalities, and public policy, often using quasi-experimental and experimental methods with large-scale global datasets. EPIC pre-doctoral alumni have gone on to top PhD programs worldwide, and several have received highly competitive National Science Foundation grants. Fellows leave the program with advanced technical skills, strong professional networks, and deep exposure to policy-relevant research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Research Focus and Methodology Research projects typically involve environmental and energy data from the United States, India, China, and other regions. Fellows apply advanced quantitative methods, computational modeling of energy markets, and policy evaluation techniques. In addition to research responsibilities, fellows participate in seminars and classes at EPIC, the University of Chicago , and partner institutions. Open Positions for the 2026 27 Academic Cycle EPIC is currently recruiting for multiple full-time, benefits-eligible Research Professional (Pre-Doctoral Fellow) roles beginning in Spring Summer 2026: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Research Professional Climate Impact Lab (Michael Greenstone) Fellows will work under the direction of Michael Greenstone , contributing to globally influential research that quantifies climate change impacts across sectors such as health, agriculture, labor, energy, conflict, migration, and coastal systems. This work supports the development of empirically derived estimates of the social cost of carbon. Salary range: $55,000 $62,000 annually Additional $2,000 professional development fund Duration: 1 2 years Expected start date: Spring Summer 2026 Applications reviewed on a rolling basis Research Professional Fiona Burlig, Erin Kelley Greg Lane This role focuses on experimental and data-intensive research in developing-country agriculture, climate adaptation, insurance markets, and climate risk forecasting, including randomized controlled trials in India. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Salary range: $55,000 $62,000 annually Additional $2,000 professional development fund Expected start date: July 1, 2026 Duration: 1 2 years APPLY HERE . For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more research fellowship opportunities here . Application Timeline and Deadlines Fall application cycle: Mid-November, with final decisions in mid-December Winter application cycle: Early March, with final decisions by late March ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Applicants must have completed a Bachelor s or Master s degree by June of the year of application and demonstrate strong quantitative and programming skills. EPIC offers a competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and an exceptional research environment that prepares fellows for top-tier doctoral programs. title: Youth, Peace and Security Online Course 2026: UN-Certified Training to Implement the YPS Agenda
summary: Build Practical Skills to Advance Youth Participation in Peace and Security Young people are increasingly recognized as powerful agents of change in preventing conflict, sustaining peace, and shaping inclusive security policies. To translate global commitments into real-world impact, practitioners need the right skills, frameworks, and tools. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); Realizing the Youth, Peace and Security Agenda is a comprehensive, practitioner-focused online course jointly developed by United Nations System Staff College and the Folke Bernadotte Academy . The programme equips professionals working in peace and security with the knowledge and applied competencies needed to effectively implement the Youth, Peace and Security (YPS) agenda while working with and for young people. Why This Course Matters The adoption of landmark UN Security Council resolutions 2250 (2015), 2419 (2018), and 2535 (2020) elevated youth participation in peace and security from aspiration to international obligation. Despite progress, implementation remains uneven across local, national, and global levels. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This course responds directly to that gap by offering a structured, skills-based learning journey that strengthens institutional capacity and supports young women and men as contributors to peaceful and just societies. Course Overview Language: English Format: Fully online Duration: Approximately 2 months of active learning (spread across 2026) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Weekly workload: 3 4 hours Course fee: USD 1,250 Enrollment Deadline: 10 April 2026 APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . The programme blends self-paced modules, live expert-led webinars, peer exchange, and written assignments. Participants enjoy flexible access via the UNSSC online learning platform, enabling learning from anywhere in the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Course Structure and Timeline Part I YPS Foundational Skills 13 April 22 May 2026 Module 1: Introduction to the Youth, Peace and Security Agenda Module 2: Enabling meaningful youth participation Module 3: Conducting youth-sensitive conflict analysis ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Includes one mandatory written assignment. Part II YPS Applied Skills (Elective Modules) 14 September 30 October 2026 Module 4: Serving as a YPS adviser or focal point Module 5: Youth-responsive and YPS-focused programming Module 6: In-country implementation of the YPS Agenda ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Includes at least one written assignment. Successful completion requires finishing all foundational modules and at least one elective module with assignments. Learning Methodology Participants engage in: Guided self-study modules Live interactive webinars (six in total) Peer learning and experience sharing Individual feedback and one-to-one facilitator support This approach ensures both conceptual understanding and practical application of YPS principles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Who Should Apply The course is designed for practitioners and representatives from: The UN system International and regional security organizations Non-governmental organizations Civil society organizations working on youth, peace, and security Selection Process Selection is competitive and based on: Quality of motivation statement Relevant experience and knowledge Commitment and availability Diversity across gender, geography, and sector Willingness to actively contribute to peer learning UN applicants must also submit a supervisor s endorsement as part of the online application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Discover more learning opportunities here . title: Transform Your Institution with the FATE Foundation AI Upskilling Programme 2026 — Empower Students and Lecturers for the AI Revolution
summary: https://wp.me/p23f03-jtXAs Artificial Intelligence reshapes global industries, African universities must lead, not follow. The FATE Foundation AI Upskilling Programme is a high-impact, continent-wide initiative designed to equip accredited Higher Education Institutions (HEIs) with world-class AI teaching capacity, research frameworks, and student readiness skills all at no cost to your institution. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); What Is the FATE Foundation AI Upskilling Programme? The AI Upskilling Programme is a multi-country initiative led by the FATE Foundation in partnership with the African Institute for Mathematical Sciences (AIMS) and supported by Google.org with a strategic $4 million investment to build a sustainable AI education ecosystem across Africa. The programme empowers institutions in: Kenya Nigeria Ghana South Africa to integrate advanced AI competencies into teaching, research, and curriculum design enabling them to produce graduates ready for tomorrow s AI-powered economy . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Who Should Apply? (Eligibility Criteria) To qualify for the 2026 intake, institutions must: 1. Be an accredited Higher Education Institution (HEI) 2. Offer STEM-related programmes (science, tech, engineering, math) 3. Demonstrate technical infrastructure readiness 4. Commit to integrating AI modules into curricula 5. Support participation from lecturers, teaching assistants, and senior STEM students 6. Participate in monitoring evaluation and capstone projects Note: Individual lecturers and students apply through their nominated institution there is no direct individual application. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Benefits for Institutions Participants 1.World-Class AI Training Participating institutions receive: Free AI training for at least four lecturers per institution. Certification for trained faculty. Localized curriculum resources for institutional use. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); 2.Curriculum Teaching Resources Curriculum modules are based on the Google DeepMind AI Research Foundation framework , covering: Small language model creation Neural network design and training Transformer architectures Model fine-tuning and alignment Real-world capstone projects This curriculum is adapted with local context to ensure relevance in African higher education systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 3.Student Empowerment Students benefit from: Advanced AI competencies aligned with global industry needs. Opportunities to build AI solutions for real-world problems. Mentorship and exposure to applied AI research. 4.Institutional Recognition Institutions gain: Visibility as innovators in AI education. Potential networking with African institutions and ecosystem partners. Contribution to Africa s developing AI ecosystem. Who Can Participate Internally? Lecturers Become certified AI Champions capable of leading instruction. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Teaching Assistants (TAs) Support AI learning and curriculum delivery. Penultimate Final-Year STEM Students Gain cutting-edge, industry-relevant AI skills. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); How to Apply Step by Step Apply here Complete the online registration form with accurate institutional and contact details. Submit before March 31 2026 (recommended). Await confirmation and further onboarding instructions. Prepare lecturers and materials for training and curriculum integration. Review eligibility guidelines and participation commitments. For questions or technical support, check the contact information on the official page or email the programme team as listed. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Key Deadlines Application Timeline Milestone Date / Status Applications Open Now Ongoing Suggested Submission Deadline 31st March 2026 (for priority review) Ongoing Review Selection Rolling basis Notifications to Selected Institutions Following review cycle Programme Implementation Window 2026 academic year Applications are reviewed periodically, and early submissions are highly recommended to secure institutional onboarding. AI skills are rapidly becoming critical in education, research, and industry. Programs like this enable African universities to lead technological innovation rather than be late adopters . This initiative is strategically positioned to: Build local expertise in AI research and teaching competencies. Strengthen technology ecosystems across multiple African nations. Advance employability and innovation capacity for students. title: WFPG Summer 2026 Internship in Washington, D.C.: Programming, Communications, and Operations Roles Now Open
summary: Applications are now open for the WFPG Summer 2026 Internship Program, offering students and emerging professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience in programming, communications, and operations. Hosted fully in person in Washington, D.C., this competitive internship provides practical exposure within a leading organization focused on global policy and leadership. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); The program is organized by the Women s Foreign Policy Group (WFPG), a respected nonprofit dedicated to advancing women s leadership in international affairs. About the WFPG Summer Internship Program The WFPG Summer 2026 Internship offers three distinct tracks: Programming Internship Communications Internship Operations Internship Applicants may apply for one or more internship tracks depending on their interests and qualifications. The program is designed to provide immersive, real-world experience in nonprofit operations and global policy engagement. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); All internships are conducted fully in person at WFPG s Washington, D.C. location, allowing interns to work closely with professionals in the field of foreign policy and international leadership. Internship Tracks Overview Programming Internship Interns support event planning, research, and coordination of high-level discussions, panels, and leadership initiatives. This track is ideal for candidates interested in international affairs, public policy, and event management. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Communications Internship Interns assist with content creation, social media management, writing, and digital outreach. This role is suitable for individuals interested in communications strategy, media engagement, and policy messaging. Operations Internship Interns contribute to administrative processes, organizational management, and internal coordination. This track is valuable for those looking to build experience in nonprofit management and operations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Why Apply? The WFPG internship program offers: Hands-on experience in a respected foreign policy nonprofit Direct exposure to international affairs programming Networking opportunities in Washington, D.C. Practical skill development in communications, operations, and policy engagement A professional in-person work environment Interns gain valuable experience that strengthens graduate school applications, policy careers, nonprofit leadership pathways, and communications roles. Location and Format Location: Washington, D.C. Format: Fully in-person Term: Summer 2026 ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Candidates must be able to work on-site in Washington, D.C. for the duration of the internship. Application Deadline Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis until March 6th at 11:59 PM ET, or until 100 applications are received, whichever comes first. Early applications are strongly encouraged due to the application cap. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Applicants should prepare their resume, cover letter, and any additional required materials before applying. How to Apply To learn more and submit your application, click here For more OFY Internship Opportunities, visit here The post WFPG Summer 2026 Internship in Washington, D.C.: Programming, Communications, and Operations Roles Now Open appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Fully Funded PhD in Social & Cultural Anthropology (Health & Environment) – KU Leuven & Makerere University | 2026 Intake
summary: A prestigious opportunity is now open for aspiring anthropologists interested in youth mental health, gambling practices, and environmental crisis in East Africa. The Department of Social and Cultural Anthropology at KU Leuven, in collaboration with Makerere University, is offering one fully funded 3-year dual PhD fellowship under the EU-funded HEALENAE Doctoral Network supported by Marie Sk odowska-Curie Actions (MSCA-DN). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(119); }); This interdisciplinary doctoral project examines the intersection between youth mental health, gambling epidemics, and environmental insecurity in East Africa. Start Date: 1 September 2026 Location: Leuven, Belgium Kampala, Uganda (Dual Degree) About KU Leuven Founded in 1425, KU Leuven is one of Europe s leading research universities. The Department of Social and Cultural Anthropology hosts a vibrant research community of lecturers, postdoctoral researchers, and PhD candidates working across global themes of making, living, and moving. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The department emphasizes long-term ethnographic research to understand how individuals and communities navigate complex social, political, and environmental realities. About the HEALENAE Project HEALENAE (Health and Environment in Africa and Europe) is an EU Horizon Europe-funded doctoral network connecting universities across: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Aarhus (Denmark) Cape Town (South Africa) Edinburgh (UK) Leuven (Belgium) Nairobi (Kenya) Oslo (Norway) Makerere University (Uganda) The programme provides: Cross-continental anthropological training One full year of fieldwork in Africa and/or Europe Annual training schools and writing retreats A 6-month stay at the secondary university Attractive salary (living + mobility allowance + family allowance if applicable) Social security and pension benefits Funding for research, travel, conferences, books, and equipment ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Research Focus: Youth Mental Health Gambling in Times of Environmental Crisis This PhD project investigates how different forms of gambling (sports betting, crypto trading, online money games, lotteries, fortune-telling) intersect with environmental and socio-economic insecurity among youth in East Africa. Key Research Objectives Map offline and online gambling practices among youth Examine historical and cultural transformations of gambling and probability practices Study how environmental instability influences risk-taking behaviors Explore gambling as a perceived strategy for securing uncertain futures Analyze risk and uncertainty in post-colonial contexts Expected Impact The research will generate: In-depth gendered and locally grounded insights Better understanding of gambling s impact on youth mental health Knowledge for prevention of gambling addiction (ludomania) Policy-relevant insights into environmental crisis and youth vulnerability ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Planned Secondments One semester at Makerere University for supervision and academic exchange Potential engagement with NGOs such as Hope for Future Generations (or similar organizations in the field site) Eligibility Criteria Applicants must: Hold an internationally recognized Master s degree (120 ECTS) in anthropology or related disciplines Graduate no later than 31 August 2026 Have less than 4 years of research experience after Master s Not hold a PhD degree Demonstrate strong academic performance Meet KU Leuven English language requirements Not have resided in Belgium for more than 12 of the last 36 months before recruitment Applicants of all nationalities are encouraged to apply. African language skills and non-academic experience are considered an asset. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Dual Degree Structure The selected candidate will: Be enrolled at both KU Leuven (Primary Institution) and Makerere University (Secondary Institution) Receive a dual PhD degree Undergo evaluation according to both universities regulations Defend publicly at KU Leuven and complete a viva at Makerere University Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Motivation letter (max 1 page, 2,400 characters) CV (education, positions, publications, activities) Research project proposal (max 12,000 characters excluding references timetable) including: Topic overview Research questions Literature discussion Methods and field sites Ethical considerations Bibliography Timetable Cover sheet Copies of academic diplomas and transcripts ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); All applications must be submitted in English. Deadline: 1 March 2026, 23:59 CET For more information click here Apply here discover scholarships opportunities here The post Fully Funded PhD in Social Cultural Anthropology (Health Environment) – KU Leuven Makerere University | 2026 Intake appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
